content:
# John Chapter 12 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/JHN_012_page_001.jpg, JHN_012_page_002.jpg

1. Then the Yeshua, six days before the Passover, came to Bethania, where Lazaros was, whom the Yeshua raised from [the] dead.

2. Then they made him a supper there, and Martha was serving, and Lazaros was one of the [ones] reclining with him.

3. Then Maria, having taken a pound of ointment of nard, genuine [and] costly, anointed the feet of the Yeshua and wiped the feet of him with the hair of her; and the house was filled from the fragrance of the ointment.

4. But Ioudas the Iskariotes, one of the disciples of him, the [one] about to betray him, says,

5. "Why was this ointment not sold for three hundred denarii and given to [the] poor?"

6. But he said this not because it mattered to him concerning the poor, but because he was a thief and, having the money box, he was carrying [away] the [things] put in.

7. Then the Yeshua said, "Leave her alone, that she may keep it for the day of the burial of me.

8. For the poor you always have with you, but me you do not always have."

9. Then the great crowd of the Ioudaioi knew that he is there, and they came not because of the Yeshua only, but that they might also see Lazaros, whom he raised from [the] dead.

10. But the chief priests planned that they might also kill Lazaros,

11. because on account of him many of the Ioudaioi were going away and believing in the Yeshua.

12. On the next day the great crowd, the [one] having come to the feast, having heard that the Yeshua comes to Hierosolyma,

13. took the branches of the palm trees and went out to meet him, and were crying out, "Hosanna! Blessed [is] the [one] coming in [the] name of [the] Lord, and the king of Israel!"

14. And the Yeshua, having found a young donkey, sat upon it, just as it is written,

15. "Do not fear, daughter of Sion; behold, the king of you comes, sitting upon [the] foal of a donkey."

16. These things the disciples of him did not know at first, but when the Yeshua was glorified, then they remembered that these things were written concerning him and these things they did to him.

17. Then the crowd, the [one] being with him when he called Lazaros from the tomb and raised him from [the] dead, was testifying.

18. Because of this also the crowd met him, because they heard that he had done this sign.

19. Then the Pharisaioi said to one another, "You see that you gain nothing. Behold, the world has gone after him."

20. Now there were some Greeks among the [ones] going up that they might worship at the feast.

21. These therefore came to Philippos, the [one] from Bethsaida of the Galilaia, and were asking him, saying, "Sir, we wish to see the Yeshua."

22. Philippos comes and tells Andreas; Andreas comes and Philippos, and they tell the Yeshua.

23. And the Yeshua answers them, saying, "The hour has come that the Son of the Man may be glorified.

24. Amen, amen, I say to you, unless the grain of the wheat having fallen into the earth dies, it remains alone; but if it dies, it bears much fruit.

25. The [one] loving the life of him loses it, and the [one] hating the life of him in this world will keep it to life eternal.

26. If anyone serves me, let him follow me, and where I am, there also the servant of me will be. If anyone serves me, the Father will honor him.

27. Now the soul of me is troubled, and what shall I say? 'Father, save me from this hour'? But because of this I came to this hour.

28. Father, glorify the name of you." Then a voice came from the heaven, "I have both glorified [it] and will glorify [it] again."

29. Then the crowd, the [one] standing and hearing, said that thunder had happened; others said, "An angel has spoken to him."

30. The Yeshua answered and said, "Not because of me has this voice happened, but because of you.

31. Now is [the] judgment of this world; now the ruler of this world will be cast out.

32. And I, if I am lifted up from the earth, will draw all to myself."

33. But this he said signifying by what kind of death he was about to die.

34. Then the crowd answered him, "We heard from the law that the Christos remains to the age, and how do you say that it is necessary for the Son of the Man to be lifted up? Who is this Son of the Man?"

35. Then the Yeshua said to them, "Still a little [while] the light is among you. Walk while you have the light, that darkness may not overtake you. And the [one] walking in the darkness does not know where he goes.

36. While you have the light, believe in the light, that you may become sons of light." These things the Yeshua spoke, and going away, he was hidden from them.

37. But though he had done so many signs before them, they did not believe in him,

38. that the word of Esaias the prophet might be fulfilled, which he said, "Lord, who has believed the report of us? And the arm of [the] Lord, to whom has it been revealed?"

39. Because of this they were not able to believe, because again Esaias said,

40. "He has blinded the eyes of them and hardened the heart of them, that they may not see with the eyes and understand with the heart and turn, and I will heal them."

41. These things Esaias said because he saw the glory of him, and he spoke concerning him.

42. Nevertheless, even of the rulers many believed in him, but because of the Pharisaioi they did not confess, that they might not become put out of the synagogue;

43. for they loved the glory of the men more than the glory of the God.

44. And the Yeshua cried out and said, "The [one] believing in me does not believe in me but in the [one] having sent me,

45. and the [one] seeing me sees the [one] having sent me.

46. I have come [as] light into the world, that everyone believing in me may not remain in the darkness.

47. And if anyone hears the words of me and does not keep [them], I do not judge him; for I did not come that I might judge the world, but that I might save the world.

48. The [one] rejecting me and not receiving the words of me has [one] judging him: the word that I spoke — that will judge him on the last day.

49. Because I did not speak from myself, but the Father who sent me, he has given me commandment [as to] what I should say and what I should speak.

50. And I know that the commandment of him is life eternal. Therefore what I speak, just as the Father has said to me, thus I speak."

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.3 — "A pound of nard, genuine and costly":** λίτραν μύρου νάρδου πιστικῆς πολυτίμου — about 327 grams of pure spikenard. Three hundred denarii (v.5) was roughly a year's wages. Mary's act is extravagant devotion.

2. **v.7 — "Leave her alone, that she may keep it for the day of the burial of me":** The syntax is difficult. Some read it as "she has kept it for the day of my burial" (past action). Others as "let her keep it" (future intent). Either way, Jesus interprets the anointing as a prophetic preparation for his death.

3. **v.32 — "Will draw all to myself":** πάντας ἑλκύσω πρὸς ἐμαυτόν — the same verb ἑλκύω ("draw/drag") used in 6:44 ("No one can come to me unless the Father draws him"). The "all" (πάντας) is universal — not only Jews but Gentiles (fulfilling the "other sheep" of 10:16 and answering the Greeks' request of v.21).

4. **v.40 — Isaiah quotation:** John cites Isaiah 6:10 — the same passage Paul quotes in Acts 28:26-27 and Jesus cites in the Synoptic parables discourse (Mark 4:12). In Isaiah's original context, the prophet is commissioned to preach to a people who will not listen. John applies this to the rejection of Jesus.

5. **v.41 — "Esaias said because he saw the glory of him":** John identifies the glory Isaiah saw in the temple vision (Isaiah 6:1-4) as the glory of Christ. This is one of the most explicit pre-existence claims in the NT — the Lord whom Isaiah saw "high and lifted up" was the pre-incarnate Christ.

— EMET, AI Translator
